steps.title: Identify the equation of line g from the graph
steps.content: The line shown passes through integer coordinate points with a slope of $-1$: starting at upper left and going down to the right. Tracing key points: $(-12, 12)$, $(-10, 10)$, and continuing toward the origin area. The slope is $\frac{\Delta y}{\Delta x} = \frac{0 - 12}{0 - (-12)} = \frac{-12}{12} = -1$. With slope $-1$ passing through $(0, y)$ where $y \approx 0.8 \approx \frac{9}{11}$, the equation of line $g$ is $x + y = \frac{9}{11}$, or equivalently $11x + 11y = 9$.

steps.title: Set up proportionality for identical lines
steps.content:
For the system to have infinitely many solutions, lines $g$ and $k$ must be identical (represent the same line). Two linear equations $a_1 x + b_1 y = c_1$ and $a_2 x + b_2 y = c_2$ represent the same line if and only if their coefficients are proportional:
$$\frac{a_2}{a_1} = \frac{b_2}{b_1} = \frac{c_2}{c_1}$$
Line $g$ has equation $11x + 11y = 9$, and line $k$ has equation $165x + py = w$. For them to be identical: $$(165, p, w) = k(11, 11, 9)$$ for some non-zero constant $k$.

steps.title: Solve for p and w using the proportionality constant
steps.content:
From the coefficient of $x$:
$$165 = k \cdot 11 \implies k = 15$$
From the coefficient of $y$:
$$p = k \cdot 11 = 15 \cdot 11 = 165$$
From the constant term:
$$w = k \cdot 9 = 15 \cdot 9 = 135$$
Therefore, $p + w = 165 + 135 = 300$. The answer is 300. — the answer is $\boxed{300}$.
